services.name: Chesapeake Regional Medical Center
services.notes: 310-bed community hospital, Level III trauma center, cardiac care, cancer center, emergency department

services.name: Sentara Norfolk General Hospital
services.notes: Level I trauma center, 525 beds, major teaching hospital, comprehensive specialty services. 20-25 min drive from Chesapeake.

services.name: CVS Pharmacy - Greenbrier
services.notes: Full-service pharmacy, drive-through, vaccinations, MinuteClinic inside

services.name: Walgreens - Battlefield Blvd
services.notes: Full-service pharmacy, drive-through, vaccinations, photo services

services.name: Food Lion - Greenbrier
services.notes: Mid-range grocery chain, competitive prices, weekly specials. Multiple locations throughout Chesapeake.

services.name: Harris Teeter - Edinburgh Commons
services.notes: Upscale grocery with deli, bakery, organic section, pharmacy. Kroger-owned.

services.name: Kroger - Great Bridge
services.notes: Full-service grocery with pharmacy, deli, fuel points. ClickList pickup available.

services.name: TowneBank - Chesapeake
services.notes: Regional bank headquartered in Hampton Roads. Full-service branch with wealth management. Multiple Chesapeake locations.

services.name: Navy Federal Credit Union - Greenbrier
services.notes: Largest credit union in US, military-affiliated but open to many. Full-service branch, ATMs.

services.name: Greenbrier Veterinary Hospital
services.notes: Full-service veterinary clinic, wellness exams, surgery, dental, boarding

services.name: Chesapeake Animal Hospital
services.notes: Small animal practice, preventive care, surgery, diagnostics

services.name: Chesapeake City Park Dog Park
services.notes: Fenced off-leash dog park within the 165-acre Chesapeake City Park. Separate areas for large and small dogs.

services.name: Northwest River Park
services.notes: 763-acre park with hiking trails, camping, canoeing. Dogs allowed on leash on trails. Nature-focused park with wildlife.

services.name: Hampton Roads Transit (HRT) - Chesapeake
services.address: Multiple stops along Battlefield Blvd and Military Hwy
services.notes: Limited bus routes in Chesapeake. Routes 15, 20, 44 connect Greenbrier and South Norfolk to Norfolk and Virginia Beach. No light rail in Chesapeake.

services.name: Norfolk International Airport (ORF)
services.notes: Closest commercial airport, 20 min drive from Chesapeake. Nonstop flights to major US hubs. Airlines include Delta, American, United, Southwest.

services.name: Planet Fitness - Greenbrier
services.notes: Budget gym, $10-25/mo, 24/7 access, tanning, massage chairs. No pool.

services.name: YMCA of South Hampton Roads - Great Bridge
services.notes: Pool, group classes, seniors programs, Silver Sneakers accepted. Family-friendly facility.

services.name: Chesapeake Central Library
services.notes: Main branch of Chesapeake Public Library system. Free WiFi, computer access, book clubs, senior programs, digital resources (Libby, Hoopla).

services.name: Chesapeake Regional Primary Care - Greenbrier
services.notes: Primary care practice, internal medicine, accepting Medicare patients, same-day sick visits available

services.name: PetSmart Grooming - Greenbrier
services.notes: Appointment and walk-in grooming, full-service baths and cuts, self-serve dog wash stations

services.name: Wegmans
services.notes: Mid-Atlantic mainstream supermarket chain.

services.name: Aldi
services.notes: Hard-discount supermarket chain — nationwide.

services.name: Best Buy
services.notes: Largest US consumer-electronics chain.

services.name: The Home Depot
services.notes: Largest US home-improvement chain.

services.notes: Women's clothing — United States chain / directory.

services.name: The Children's Place
services.notes: Children's clothing — United States chain / directory.

services.name: Destination XL
services.notes: Big & Tall clothing — United States chain / directory.

attractions.name: Great Dismal Swamp National Wildlife Refuge
attractions.description: 112,000-acre refuge with hiking trails, wildlife viewing, kayaking on Lake Drummond, and rich biodiversity
attractions.frequency: Daily sunrise to sunset, free admission. Best seasons: spring and fall.

sources.title: US Fish & Wildlife - Great Dismal Swamp

attractions.name: Northwest River Park
attractions.description: 763-acre park with hiking trails, campgrounds, canoeing, fishing, and nature programs
attractions.frequency: Daily dawn to dusk, free admission. Camping and boat rentals available for a fee.

sources.title: City of Chesapeake - Northwest River Park

attractions.name: Chesapeake City Park
attractions.description: 165-acre park with trails, playgrounds, athletic fields, disc golf, dog park, and picnic areas
attractions.frequency: Daily dawn to dusk, free admission

attractions.name: Virginia Beach Oceanfront
attractions.description: 3-mile boardwalk, sandy beach, restaurants, shops, and seasonal events. 30 minutes from Chesapeake.
attractions.frequency: Year-round, free beach access. Parking fees apply in summer.

attractions.name: Great Bridge Battlefield & Waterways History Museum
attractions.description: Site of the 1775 Battle of Great Bridge, a key early American Revolution victory. Museum and walking trails.

sources.title: City of Chesapeake - Great Bridge Battlefield

attractions.name: Chrysler Museum of Art (Norfolk)
attractions.description: World-class art museum with 30,000+ objects spanning 5,000 years. Glass studio with live glassblowing.

attractions.name: Greenbrier Mall
attractions.description: Regional shopping mall with department stores, retail shops, food court, and movie theater

attractions.name: Norfolk Naval Station Tours
attractions.description: Largest naval base in the world. Bus tours available through Naval Station Norfolk. View aircraft carriers and ships.
attractions.frequency: Seasonal tours, typically spring-fall, reservations required

attractions.name: First Landing State Park (Virginia Beach)
attractions.description: 2,888-acre state park at the mouth of the Chesapeake Bay with trails through cypress swamps and sandy beaches
attractions.frequency: Daily dawn to dusk, parking fee $7-10
